channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Enforcement Directorate raids Kolkata Police DCP's residence and arrests real estate businessman in money laundering case linked to illegal land deals
item.description: The Enforcement Directorate (ED) conducted raids on Sunday at the residence of Shantanu Sinha Biswas, a senior Deputy Commissioner of Police (Special Branch) in Kolkata Police, as part of a money laun...

item.title: Eight children die in Louisiana domestic shooting, gunman fatally shot after police chase
item.description: In a tragic incident in Shreveport, Louisiana, eight children were killed in what police are describing as a domestic violence-related shooting spree. The gunman, an adult male, shot and killed childr...

item.title: Polling indicates decline in One Nation support as voters shift back to major parties
item.description: New polling suggests that support for Pauline Hanson’s One Nation party has declined, with voters appearing to move back towards Australia’s major political parties. While the article does not provide...

item.title: Iranian Green Card Holder Arrested at LAX for Allegedly Brokering Arms Sales from Iran to Sudan
item.description: Shamim Mafi, a 44-year-old Iranian immigrant and U.S. green card holder living in Woodland Hills, California, was arrested Saturday night, April 19, 2026, at Los Angeles International Airport. Federal...

item.title: Director Morgan Neville Explains Pete Davidson's Absence from Lorne Michaels Documentary Due to Scheduling and Focus
item.description: In the new documentary 'Lorne,' director Morgan Neville provides an intimate look into the life and career of Saturday Night Live creator Lorne Michaels. During an exclusive interview with PEOPLE, Nev...

item.title: Counter-terror police investigate attempted arson attack at north-west London synagogue
item.description: Counter-terrorism police are investigating an attempted arson attack at a synagogue in north-west London, according to the Community Security Trust and Sky News reports. The incident follows a series ...

item.title: CBI Arrests DGCA Official and Private Executive in Rs 2.5 Lakh Bribery Case Linked to Drone Licence
item.description: The Central Bureau of Investigation (CBI) has arrested a senior official from the Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA) along with a senior executive from a private company in a bribery case in...

item.title: Independent Roundup of Global Nuclear Developments, Safety Concerns, and Geopolitical Impacts
item.description: This weekly compilation gathers independent and non-mainstream coverage of nuclear industry issues, safety challenges, and related geopolitical events. Key highlights include criticism of the U.S. Nuc...

item.title: Delhi unveils semiconductor-focused policy to position itself as a chip manufacturing hub

item.title: Australia joins international efforts for permanent reopening of Strait of Hormuz as Albanese calls arrangement fragile
item.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has attended a virtual summit hosted by France and the United Kingdom with around 50 countries to discuss the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. The vital waterway has ...

item.title: La Salle Lady Spikers finish elimination round undefeated with 14-0 record and advance straight to UAAP Season 88 finals
item.description: In a dominant performance during UAAP Season 88 women's volleyball tournament, the La Salle Lady Spikers completed a perfect 14-0 sweep of the elimination round. This historic achievement grants them ...

item.title: Baggage Handling System Failure at KLIA Causes Multi-Hour Delays; Malaysia Transport Minister Orders Investigation

item.title: Self-Healing Concrete: Modern Bacterial Solutions and Ancient Roman Innovations
item.description: Modern researchers have developed self-healing concrete by embedding bacteria capable of surviving harsh conditions along with their nutrients inside the mix. These bacteria remain dormant until crack...

item.title: Rising water levels threaten homes and roads in Gatineau amid spring flooding in Quebec and Ontario
item.description: Spring flooding conditions are causing significant concern across parts of Quebec and Ontario, with Gatineau among the hardest-hit areas as water levels continue to rise along the Ottawa River. Local ...

item.title: Manchester City Defeat Arsenal 2-1 in Premier League Title Race Clash at Etihad
item.description: Naija fans, make una hear this one! Manchester City just beat Arsenal 2-1 for one very hot Premier League match wey happen for Etihad Stadium on Sunday, April 19, 2026. The game na real title decider ...

item.title: PM Shehbaz’s Diplomatic Visit Draws Attention for Both Policy Efforts and Personal Presentation
item.description: Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif recently completed a high-level four-day diplomatic tour covering Saudi Arabia, Qatar, and Turkiye from April 15 to 18. The visit was primarily focused on sensitive geopo...

item.title: Restorative justice helps former addict rebuild life after involvement in Middlesbrough riots
item.description: Stacey Vint, a 34-year-old mother of five from Middlesbrough, was captured on video pushing a burning wheelie bin towards police lines during far-right riots that followed the Southport attack in 2024...

item.title: Human Societies as a Transformative Force in the Anthropocene: Balancing Progress and Sustainability
item.description: Human societies have evolved from merely adapting to the natural world to actively reshaping it on a massive scale through cultural and social innovations. From the early controlled use of fire to mod...

item.title: Gunman and 12-year-old girl killed in General Santos shootout; fruit vendor slain in Cotabato grenade attack

item.title: Nigeria Police Redeploys CP Akin Fakorede to Head Anti-Fraud Unit at FCID Alagbon Amid Public Criticism
